backend: mlx-lm 0.31.3 (LoRA SFT; no DPO trainer in this version — binding fact)
surface: freshly-generated sympy-checkable arithmetic/linear math; train seed1&3, held-out seed2; exact-overlap=0
objective: W2 proper-scoring calibration wired as calibration-SFT: target Confidence = verifier-measured per-kind train accuracy (difficulty-adaptive); measured by agent.calibration.expected_calibration_error
gate: ECE down on held-out verifier-checkable eval at matched-or-better accuracy-at-coverage (no hedging collapse), reproduced across >=2 seeds on one pinned commit.
gateVerdict: CANDIDATE / NOT-CLEARED. Both seeds lower held-out ECE (0.200 -> 0.058/0.087) at matched-or-better accuracy AND accuracy@coverage improves 0.75 -> 1.00 (no hedging collapse). BUT seed1's per-seed ECE-reduction 95% CI includes 0 (N=80 underpowered) and the surface is a narrow type-level-confidence math generator, not general calibration. w2 ledger row STAYS OPEN.
nextToClose: Larger held-out N (tighten CI so each seed excludes 0), a >=3rd seed, and a broader non-math verifier-checkable surface (open-domain factual QA with a real answer-verifier) to show calibration beyond a controlled difficulty taxonomy.
